title: Monthly Marketing Review
summary: A monthly operating review to decide what is working, what is wasting budget, and what the team should double down on next month.
whenToUseIt: Use this in the first week of every month after your core channel data and spend are finalised.

steps.title: Pull the monthly scorecard
steps.description: Gather channel spend, traffic, leads, conversion rates, CAC, and pipeline or revenue contribution in one place. Use the same metrics every month so trend lines are obvious.

steps.title: Review channel efficiency
steps.description: Compare each channel by spend, volume, and outcome quality. Flag channels that are generating activity without meaningful business impact.
steps.tips: Do not reward volume that does not translate into pipeline, retention, or revenue.

steps.title: Check campaign quality
steps.description: Look at launch performance, messaging resonance, landing page conversion, and audience fit across your major campaigns from the month.

steps.title: Decide what to stop, scale, and fix
steps.description: Write down one thing to stop, one thing to scale, and one thing to repair in the next cycle. Tie each decision to a clear owner.

steps.title: Set the next-month operating priorities
steps.description: Choose the 2-3 marketing priorities for the upcoming month and translate them into budget, content, and campaign actions.
steps.aiPrompt: Here is my monthly marketing scorecard: [paste metrics]. Identify the strongest channel, the weakest channel, one likely bottleneck, and the top three priorities for next month.

expectedOutcome: A clear monthly view of marketing performance, three concrete operating decisions, and a tighter plan for the next cycle.
commonMistakes: Reviewing vanity metrics without connecting them to business impact
Letting every channel continue because no one wants to kill work in progress
Skipping the review when results are bad and losing the chance to learn quickly
Changing the scorecard every month so trends cannot be trusted
